ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class; no Sichuan chicken dumpling-specific entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW covers meat-filled dumplings as the closest defensible category; no Sichuan-specific code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Sichuan chicken dumpling; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

preparation_method: served with soya sauce and chili oil

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 5 dumplings, weighing approximately 150 g, typically served with soya sauce and chili oil on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Sichuan Chicken Dumpling — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sichuan Chicken Dumpling (cr_id G00010687). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for boiled wheat-flour dumplings / dim sum
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Sichuan Chicken Dumpling. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on the closest analogous entry in the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat-flour dumplings / dim sum with meat filling. The wheat-flour wrapper is the primary glycaemic driver; the high protein content (8.71 g/100g) and low sugar (0.21 g/100g) further moderate the glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (12.05 g for 150 g serving) / 100 = 5.4.

hypertension.guidance: The dumpling itself is very low in sodium (46 mg/100g); patients with hypertension should limit or avoid the accompanying soya sauce and chili oil dipping sauce, which is the primary source of sodium in this dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.03 g/100g), high protein (8.71 g/100g), and very low sugar (0.21 g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The favourable protein-to-carbohydrate ratio and very low sugar content support glycaemic stability; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should control portion size (limit to 5 pieces per serving) and avoid sweet dipping sauces that add additional sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.78 g/100g), low saturated fat (~0.9 g/100g), and very low sodium (46 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat and sodium profile makes this dish suitable for cardiovascular disease management when consumed without excessive chili oil or soya sauce dipping; steamed preparation is preferred over pan-fried to minimise added fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of meaningful anti-inflammatory ingredients at therapeutic quantities; chili oil contains nightshade-family chilli
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish does not meet the positive anti-inflammatory criterion for rheumatoid arthritis suitability; individuals with RA should monitor for joint flares associated with nightshade consumption (chilli in the oil) and consider requesting extra ginger or turmeric in the filling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sichuan Chicken Dumpling (四川鸡肉饺子) is a Chinese-origin dish popular in Singapore, consisting of five boiled or steamed wheat-flour dumplings filled with seasoned minced chicken, served with a dipping sauce of soya sauce and chili oil. The wrapper is thin and slightly chewy, while the filling is tender and savoury with mild heat from the Sichuan-style chili oil. Per 100g, the dish provides 101 kcal, 8.71g protein, 3.78g fat, and 8.03g carbohydrate, with very low sugar (0.21g) and exceptionally low sodium (46mg) in the dumpling itself — though the dipping sauce significantly raises sodium intake. The high protein-to-carbohydrate ratio makes this dish suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension when the dipping sauce is controlled. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low), driven by the wheat-flour wrapper moderated by the protein-rich filling. Clinically, the dish is low in saturated fat and sodium, making it broadly suitable for cardiovascular health. It is not recommended for rheumatoid arthritis patients without modification due to the presence of nightshade-derived chilli and the absence of meaningful anti-inflammatory ingredients at therapeutic quantities. The dish is a common lunch and dinner option in Singapore's Chinese food landscape.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Sichuan chicken dumpling?
What is the GI of chicken dumplings?
Is Sichuan dumpling suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010687) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. All core macros and key micronutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Sichuan Chicken Dumpling preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010687) as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 0.9 g/100g and dietary fibre at 0.5 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central closest analogous entry for boiled chicken dumplings (USDA SR Legacy), as these fields were not reported in the HPB source; this adaptation is documented here. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat-flour dumplings with meat filling; no direct GI measurement for Sichuan Chicken Dumpling was found in any approved source. Halal status is not confirmed — the dish contains chicken and soya sauce; halal certification depends on the specific preparation and supplier. All core macros (protein_g 8.71, fat_g 3.78, carbohydrate_g 8.03) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required.
